description: ANGELOS messenger known-answer tests (design: spec/platform.md §6). ANGELOS is the platform's single-face app — chat, calls, communities, and the in-conversation wallet. These vectors pin the language-agnostic wire formats every per-language messaging-bot SDK must reproduce byte-for-byte to interoperate: the content-plane message envelope, the bot command grammar, and the three crypto planes (1:1 session, sender-key group, real-time media). All seals are the one shared ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD (wire.json / spec §7.1) under a 32-byte key and a counter-derived 12-byte nonce; all key derivations are BLAKE3 under the domain labels below (hash_labeled(label, input) = BLAKE3 keyed by the 32-byte BLAKE3 hash of the ASCII label). Pinned by the Rust reference suite (fanos-angelos/tests/conformance.rs); change the implementation and the tests fail, so the vectors cannot drift. Byte strings are lower-case hex; integers are decimal unless noted.

nonce.comment: Every ANGELOS AEAD nonce is a per-key monotonic counter n placed little-endian in the low 8 bytes of the 12-byte nonce, the high 4 bytes zero. Each key seals a single numbered stream, so (key, nonce) is unique — the AEAD safety requirement.
nonce.layout: u64 counter little-endian in bytes 0..8, bytes 8..12 = 0x00

message.comment: The plaintext content-plane envelope an SDK sees after decryption. Canonical layout: channel(32) || sender(32) || seq(8, little-endian) || kind(1) || content_len(4, little-endian u32) || content. A decoder MUST reject trailing bytes past content_len and any unknown kind tag.

vectors.comment: A text post. seq shown to exercise the little-endian width.
A payment carried in-chat (the wallet lives in the conversation): kind=Payment(6), content is a serialized value transfer the app's wallet built (a stub 'pay:100' here).

command.comment: A bot command is a text message whose UTF-8 body starts with the command prefix (default '/'). The first whitespace token (sans prefix) is the name; the rest are whitespace-separated args. A prefix with no name, or text without the prefix, is not a command.

attachment.comment: A file pointer carried as the content of an Attachment message (kind 9). The file is sealed and stored in THESAUROS; the descriptor rides inside the E2E-encrypted message, so the key is safe. Layout: cid(32) || key(32) || size(8, little-endian) || media_type_len(2, little-endian) || media_type(utf-8). A decoder rejects trailing bytes and non-UTF-8.

call.comment: Call signaling — the content of a CallSignal message that sets up a voice/video call. Canonical layout: tag(1) || call_id(16), and for an Invite || media_secret(32) || media_flags(1). The caller sends an Invite carrying a fresh media secret (the media session's epoch-0 key) and the offered media; both sides seed an identical media session from the secret. A decoder MUST reject an unknown tag, a short body, or trailing bytes.

session.comment: The 1:1 forward-secret ratchet. From a 32-byte shared secret (agreed by the KEM handshake, a group rekey, or a PSK): root = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/root', shared); a2b = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/a2b', root); b2a = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/b2a', root). The initiator sends on a2b, the responder on b2a. Each message: mk = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/mk', chain); chain' = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/next', chain); wire = n(8, little-endian) || AEAD(mk, nonce(n), plaintext). The chain is one-way, so a compromised key never opens past messages.

vector.comment: Initiator over shared secret = 0x01 repeated 32 times, sealing 'gm' twice; the same plaintext seals differently as the send chain (a2b) ratchets.

group.comment: The sender-key group session (an O(1) post to a channel). Each member derives their own sender chain from the shared group key and their public member id: sender_chain = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/group-sender', group_key(32) || member_id(4, little-endian)). A post advances the poster's chain: mk = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/group-mk', chain); chain' = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/group-next', chain); wire = n(8, little-endian) || AEAD(mk, nonce(n), plaintext). Every other member reproduces the poster's chain from the group key and opens with it.

vector.comment: Member id 1 in roster [1,2,3] over group key = 0x42 repeated 32 times, posting 'hello channel' twice.

media.comment: The real-time voice/video session — loss-tolerant per-frame AEAD under a per-call, PER-DIRECTION epoch key. The two ends derive DISTINCT send keys so their seq=0 frames never collide on a (key, nonce) pair (sharing one key across both directions is a ChaCha20-Poly1305 nonce reuse — a two-time pad). caller epoch-0 key = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/media-epoch0-caller', call_secret); callee epoch-0 key = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/media-epoch0-callee', call_secret); each party seals under its own direction's key and opens under the peer's. A rekey advances each key' = H('FANOS-angelos-v1/media-next-epoch', key) for forward secrecy across epochs. Each frame: inner = kind(1) || payload; wire = epoch(4, little-endian) || seq(8, little-endian) || AEAD(dir_key, nonce(seq), inner). Any frame of the current epoch opens on its own, in any order.

vector.comment: Epoch 0, CALLER direction, over call secret = 0x33 repeated 32 times: an audio frame at seq 0, then a video frame at seq 1 (opened by the callee under the caller's key). Distinct per-direction keys mean the callee's seq-0 frame differs, so the two directions never reuse a (key, nonce).
